MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Lift Stay
Off Calendar Nov-12-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARK J. TEREBA'S Motion To Lift Stay is denied. Plaintiffs have filed several similar wage and hour putative class actions against Dignity Health hospitals in California. The first-filed is Allison in this court (CGC-18-566922), in which a class certification motion is set for hearing January 7. Plaintiff here points to a scope change in the second-filed action. However, Dignity points to four other actions filed before Tereba (three in this court), all of which are stayed.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2021 Continued To Jan-26-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action On Grounds Of Other Similar Actions Pending With Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH's Motion To Stay Action On Grounds Of Other Similar Actions Pending.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to stay is granted. The Court has inherent power to stay a duplicative action. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 128(a)(8); OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141 ["A court ordinarily has inherent power, in its discretion, to stay proceedings when a stay will accommodate the ends of justice."]. The Court finds that the instant action overlaps with the Vakil (Case No. CGC-18-569456) and Allison (Case No. CGC-18-566922) actions and a stay is warranted to avoid duplication and to further the ends of justice. (See Thomson v. Continental Ins. Co. (1967) 66 Cal.2d 738, 746 ["Granting a stay in a case where the issues in two actions are substantially [similar]" is thus "a matter addressed to the sound direction of the trial court."].) Plaintiff cites no authority that the Court lacks the power to entertain this motion because defendant has yet to demur or answer.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
